info.name: Vulcan API latest
info.description: Vulcan Project API - Models, Metrics, Lineage, and Telemetry

request.description:
List all quality checks with their latest execution status.

Returns a catalog of all unique quality checks that have been executed, including check metadata
(identity, name, type, dimension, definition) and the most recent execution result. Each check's
`runs` array contains only the latest run (`runs[0]` if exists), showing the current status.

Use query parameters to filter checks by `model_name` (comma-separated), `column_name`, or `dimension`.
When filtering by `column_name` alone, returns checks for that column across all models. Combine
with `model_name` to scope the column filter to specific models. This provides a check-first view
of quality data, useful for discovering all checks across models and understanding their current
execution status.

To see execution history for a specific check, use the `/quality/checks/{check_identity}` endpoint.

response.name: Quality checks retrieved successfully

request.description:
Get execution history for a specific quality check.

Returns the complete execution history of a check across multiple runs, showing how its outcome
changed over time. The `runs` array contains all executions ordered by most recent first, with
each entry including run metadata (`run_id`, `start_ts`, `end_ts`) and check outcome (`pass`/`fail`/`warn`/`error`).

Use query parameters to filter runs by `model_name`, time range (`start_ts`, `end_ts`), or `limit` the
number of results. This is useful for tracking check reliability over time, identifying when a
check started failing or passing, and analyzing patterns in check behavior.

To list all checks with their latest status, use the `/quality/checks` endpoint.

response.name: Check runs retrieved successfully
Check not found or has no runs

request.description:
Get product-level quality snapshot at a specific point in time.

Returns aggregated quality metrics across all models in the product, computed using the latest
known quality for each model up to the specified run. Quality metrics are grouped by dimension
(e.g., completeness, accuracy, timeliness) and include pass/fail/warn/error counts and pass rates.

Use `latest` as `run_id` to get the most recent snapshot, or provide a specific `run_id` for historical
time-travel queries. For each model, the system uses the most recent quality data available up to
the target run (models may have quality from different runs), then aggregates across all models.

The response includes `prev_run_id` and `next_run_id` fields for navigating backward and forward
through snapshots chronologically. The `models_included` field lists all models that contributed
to this snapshot. Quality metrics represent the entire product, not filtered to specific models.

Examples: `/quality/snapshot/latest` for the most recent snapshot, `/quality/snapshot/abc123` for
a specific historical point in time.

variable.description: (Required) Run ID for time-travel. Use 'latest' for the most recent snapshot.

response.name: No quality data found for the specified criteria

variable.description: (Required) Run ID for time-travel. Use 'latest' for the most recent snapshot.
(Required) Run ID for time-travel. Use 'latest' for the most recent snapshot.

request.description:
Get unified timeline of plan changes and run executions.

Returns a chronological list of all plan and run activities ordered by creation time (most recent first).
Each event includes basic metadata and links to its detail page. Run events also include aggregated
quality metrics by dimension (pass/fail/warn counts and pass rates) for all models executed in that run.

Use query parameters to filter by `environment`, `action` (plan/run), `after_start_ts` (time range),
or `model_name` (comma-separated model names). When filtering by `model_name`, only activities
affecting those models are included, but quality metrics in run events still show all models executed in
each run (not filtered to the specified models).

Plan events show what changed (models added/removed/modified). Run events show execution results and
quality metrics. Use the `_links.self` field in each event to navigate to detailed information.
Supports pagination via `limit` and `offset` parameters.

response.name: Timeline events retrieved successfully
Model not found (when filtering by model_name)

request.description:
Get detailed information for a specific plan deployment.

Returns complete plan information including what changed (added, removed, modified models),
which models were affected directly and indirectly, backfill requirements, execution metadata,
and links to related plans. Use this to understand what a plan deployment changed and its impact.

To list all plans, use the `/activity/timeline` endpoint with `action=plan` filter.

Get complete information for a specific run execution.

Returns unified run information including execution status, performance metrics for all executed models,
quality check results (if available), and data profiling statistics (if available). Quality checks and
profiles are grouped by model name and include all models executed in the run.

Quality and profile fields are `None` if no quality checks or profiling were performed for the run.
This is not an error condition - it simply means those features weren't enabled for that execution.

To list all runs, use the `/activity/timeline` endpoint with `action=run` filter.

response.name: Run details retrieved successfully

request.description:
Get execution history for a specific model.

Returns a paginated list of runs that executed the specified model, including execution metrics,
quality checks, and profile data filtered to that model. Returns the same structure as
`/activity/runs/{run_id}`, but filtered to show only the specified model's execution details.

Run-level metrics (`start_ts`, `end_ts`, `success`, `rows_affected`, `evaluation_ms`)
represent the entire run execution, not just this model. Use query parameters `start_ts` and `end_ts`
to filter by time range, or `limit` and `offset` to control pagination.

To get complete run information for all models in a run, use `/activity/runs/{run_id}`.

response.name: Model runs retrieved successfully

request.description: Returns statement status (ACCEPTED, QUEUED, IN_PROGRESS, SUCCESS, FAILED). Poll this endpoint until SUCCESS or FAILED.
Supports parquet (redirect), json, yaml, csv. Text formats support limit/offset/columns filtering.

Submit source SQL query for execution.

Accepts raw SQL queries and submits them for asynchronous execution via the
statement flow. Returns immediately with a statement ID for polling. Validates SQL,
checks cache, and submits to queue. Always returns `202 Accepted` with statement ID.
Use `_links` to poll `GET /statement/{id}` for status and results.

response.name: Statement accepted; use `_links` to poll status and fetch results via `GET /statement/{id}`.

request.description:
Submit REST semantic query for execution.

Accepts REST Query format (measures, dimensions, filters) and converts it to
SQL before submitting for execution via the statement flow. Converts REST query
to SQL, then executes via statement flow. Always returns `202 Accepted`. Use `_links`
to poll `GET /statement/{id}` for status and results.

response.name: Statement accepted; use `_links` to poll status and fetch results via `GET /statement/{id}`.
Invalid REST semantic query or SQL generation failed

request.description:
Submit REST semantic query for execution.

Accepts REST Query format (measures, dimensions, filters) and converts it to
SQL before submitting for execution via the statement flow. Converts REST query
to SQL, then executes via statement flow. Always returns `202 Accepted`. Use `_links`
to poll `GET /statement/{id}` for status and results.

response.name: Statement accepted; use `_links` to poll status and fetch results via `GET /statement/{id}`.
Invalid REST semantic query or SQL generation failed

request.description:
Submit semantic SQL query for execution.

Accepts semantic SQL (SQL with metric/dimension references) and converts it to
executable SQL before submitting for execution via the statement flow. Converts
semantic SQL to executable SQL, then executes via statement flow. Always returns
202 Accepted. Use `_links` to poll `GET /statement/{id}` for status and results.

For MySQL client dialect transpilation, use the internal endpoint `/_internal/query/semantic/sql`.

response.name: Statement accepted; use `_links` to poll status and fetch results via `GET /statement/{id}`.
Invalid semantic SQL query or compilation failed

request.description:
Submit semantic SQL query for execution.

Accepts semantic SQL (SQL with metric/dimension references) and converts it to
executable SQL before submitting for execution via the statement flow. Converts
semantic SQL to executable SQL, then executes via statement flow. Always returns
202 Accepted. Use `_links` to poll `GET /statement/{id}` for status and results.

For MySQL client dialect transpilation, use the internal endpoint `/_internal/query/semantic/sql`.

response.name: Statement accepted; use `_links` to poll status and fetch results via `GET /statement/{id}`.
Invalid semantic SQL query or compilation failed

request.description:
Get complete structural metadata of the data product.

Returns the full metadata catalog including the product definition, all models with their
schemas and properties, all metrics with their definitions, and a graph structure representing
relationships between entities. This is the primary discovery endpoint for understanding what
data assets exist in the system and how they relate to each other.

response.name: Successful Response

request.description:
Kubernetes liveness probe endpoint.

Returns a simple health check response indicating the API is alive and responding.
Used by Kubernetes to determine if the container should be restarted.

Kubernetes readiness probe endpoint.

Returns a simple health check response indicating the API is ready to accept traffic.
Used by Kubernetes to determine if the container should receive requests.
